_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Marcelo Mayer struggling with changeups: hitting .029 against them since call-up
items.snark: Top prospect can't hit changeups and looks lost on low pitches, but sure, skip Triple-A entirely.

items.headline: Franklin Arias now top Sox prospect but has played just 36 of 44 games at Portland
items.snark: New top prospect can't stay healthy in Double-A, which is exactly the kind of luck this org deserves right now.

items.headline: Red Sox open 3-game set with Braves tonight as Ranger Suarez takes the mound
items.snark: Suarez back from his hammy just in time to watch the bats go catatonic against Atlanta pitching.

items.headline: Red Sox swept at Fenway by Twins, now 8-17 at home with worst home record in MLB
items.snark: Getting swept at home twice before Memorial Day is a vibe, if that vibe is sadness mixed with expensive parking.

items.headline: Reality check: Red Sox are 22-30, tied for wild card spot 3.5 games out despite disaster
items.snark: Being in the playoff race at 22-30 says more about the AL being cooked than the Sox being good.

items.headline: Patriots OTAs begin Wednesday: Drake Maye leadership, O-line battles to watch
items.snark: Year 3 Maye needs to be a vocal leader now that he's survived the Super Bowl gauntlet, no pressure kid.

items.headline: Robert Spillane set tone on defense last year, but who starts next to him at LB?
items.snark: Spillane didn't miss a snap all year but finding his running mate is still a mystery heading into OTAs.

items.headline: A.J. Brown trade rumors heat up with June 1 approaching, cap hit cuts in half
items.snark: June 1 is burned into Pats and Eagles brains like a bad tattoo, but Philly's asking price still reportedly unserious.
